caveat: Deterministic evaluation of DECLARED rules over agent-semantic events, per AgentSpec's model — not intent understanding: behaviour no rule anticipates proceeds, so pair with a default-deny tool policy (the tool_guard module) when the baseline must be an allowlist. Enforcement here is a decision; carrying it out (stopping the agent, invoking the user, MI9-style graduated containment) is the surrounding runtime's job.
statement: The vendored runtime rule engine passes all 22 checks with 0 mismatches: AgentSpec's four enforcement kinds (user_inspection, llm_self_examine, invoke_action, stop) are encoded verbatim and any other — including 'skip', which the paper does not define — is rejected; 12 deterministic-semantics checks hold (first-match-wins verified under rule permutation, Decimal-exact numeric boundaries where 5000 is not > 5000 but "5000.01" is, missing attributes failing predicates closed, malformed events failing closed to stop); the trace evaluator halts exactly at a stop verdict; and every one of 7 single-field mutations of a matching event flips the verdict (mutations_missed = 0).
